name: nyx
description: Self-contained script for cleaning forensic traces on Linux, macOS, and Windows.
readme_summary: Nyx is a self-contained shell script designed to clean forensic traces across Linux, macOS, and Windows operating systems. It systematically removes artifacts related to shell history, logs, network activity, user data, browsers, and various applications to help maintain system privacy and security.
key_features: Cross-platform support (Linux, macOS, Windows)
Comprehensive artifact cleaning (shell history, logs, network, user data, browsers, applications)
Modular design for targeted cleaning
Self-contained script for ease of use
Alpha software with ongoing development
primary_use_case: Cleaning forensic traces and sensitive data from operating systems to enhance privacy and security.
cover_image_prompt: A mystical night spirit, Nyx, cloaked in shadows and starlight, gently sweeping away digital footprints and data trails from a stylized, multi-layered representation of computer systems (Linux, macOS, Windows). Nyx's touch causes the traces – represented as glowing, ephemeral wisps of data, browser histories, and log entries – to dissipate into harmless digital dust. Subtle UI elements showing 'cleaning' progress and a small 'Nyx' logo are integrated into the scene. The overall mood is serene yet powerful, emphasizing the act of erasure and privacy. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a dark, atmospheric color palette accented by glowing digital elements.

name: ShizuWall
description: Lightweight no root, no vpn firewall solution powered by Shizuku
readme_summary: ShizuWall is a lightweight Android firewall application that allows users to block network connections for specific apps without needing root access or a VPN. It utilizes the Shizuku service or a local ADB daemon to manage network permissions on a per-app basis, offering a privacy-focused solution.
key_features: No root or VPN required
Per-app system networking control
Privacy-first design
Leverages Shizuku or local ADB daemon
primary_use_case: To provide users with granular control over application network access on Android devices without requiring root privileges or a VPN connection, enhancing privacy and reducing data usage.
cover_image_prompt: A digital illustration of a vigilant Android robot standing guard at a gate. On one side of the gate, numerous app icons are trying to pass through, some glowing red with a 'blocked' symbol. The robot is holding a shield that displays a subtle 'ShizuWall' logo and is selectively allowing only specific, 'green-lit' app icons to pass through the gate. The background is a clean, abstract digital space with subtle network lines. The overall mood is secure and controlled. Style: 3D isometric illustration with clear iconography and a modern, clean aesthetic.

name: dline
description: A sleek and simple calendar for terminal enthusiasts.
readme_summary: dLine is a command-line calendar tool designed for terminal enthusiasts. It allows users to view monthly calendars, track events, calculate timespans between dates, and manage multiple calendar datasets directly from their terminal.
key_features: Dynamic calendar view with color-coded events and filtering
Static monthly calendar view
Event calculator for workdays and timespans
Calendar data management (add, delete, update, import, export)
Support for multiple calendars and public holidays
Interactive event adding and updating
primary_use_case: Managing personal schedules and important dates directly within the terminal environment.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, minimalist terminal window displaying a vibrant, color-coded calendar. Inside the terminal, a small, glowing digital hourglass is actively counting down days between two abstract date markers. Subtle data streams flow from the sides of the terminal into the calendar, representing event data. The overall mood is efficient and organized, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The art style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on glowing digital elements and clear, readable calendar elements within the terminal frame. The repository name 'dLine' is subtly integrated into the terminal's title bar.

name: sablier
description: Start your containers on demand, shut them down automatically when there's no activity. Docker, Docker Swarm Mode and Kubernetes compatible.
readme_summary: Sablier is an open-source tool designed to manage containerized workloads by starting them only when needed and automatically shutting them down after a period of inactivity. It integrates with various reverse proxies and orchestration platforms like Docker, Docker Swarm, and Kubernetes to optimize resource usage.
key_features: Starts containers on demand
Automatically shuts down inactive containers
Docker, Docker Swarm Mode, and Kubernetes compatible
Integrates with reverse proxies (Traefik, Caddy, Nginx, Envoy, etc.)
Provides a waiting page for users while containers start
Optimizes resource usage for cost savings and efficiency
primary_use_case: To reduce resource consumption and operational costs by automatically managing the lifecycle of containerized applications, ensuring they are only active when actively being used.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical scene depicting a 'sleeping' server rack, represented as a cozy bed with a blanket, gently snoring. Suddenly, a user's request (visualized as a glowing digital spark) arrives, causing one of the servers to 'wake up' with a gentle stretch, indicated by a soft light emanating from it. A small, friendly robot labeled 'Sablier' is shown monitoring the rack, with a timer icon above it that resets when new requests arrive. The background is a clean, modern data center with subtle code snippets floating in the air. The overall mood is efficient and peaceful. Style: 3D isometric illustration with soft, glowing lighting and a clean, minimalist color palette.
